title: DisplayMessage uses twin boolean flags (is_error, is_thinking) — replace with enum status
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Structural Modularity (Boolean Flag Parameter) / Cognitive Readability
Location: src/tui/messages.rs:10-16 and every DisplayMessage literal (30+ call sites across src/tui/app.rs)
Violation: DisplayMessage { role: String, content: String, tool_name: Option<String>, is_error: bool, is_thinking: bool } encodes four orthogonal states (normal, error, thinking, tool-result) as two independent booleans. The combination (is_error=true, is_thinking=true) is nonsensical but representable. Every construction site repeats 'is_error: false, is_thinking: false' boilerplate. This is the boolean-flag anti-pattern applied to data carriers.
Mandated Refactor: Replace with: pub enum MessageKind { User, Assistant, AssistantThinking, SystemInfo, SystemError, ToolStart { name: String }, ToolOk { name: String }, ToolErr { name: String } }. Then pub struct DisplayMessage { kind: MessageKind, content: String }. This makes invalid states unrepresentable and eliminates ~120 lines of 'role: "system".to_string(), tool_name: None, is_error: false, is_thinking: false' boilerplate in app.rs. The match arms in messages.rs::build_lines become exhaustive and self-documenting.
